meta.description: Starter asset graph for the US crude-oil prototype. Production, infrastructure, and refinery layers all loaded; closes US refining capacity to 100%. Canadian-supply layer included (oil-sands aggregate + 4 cross-border pipelines).

nodes.name: Permian (Texas portion)
nodes.notes: Physical sub-node; activated when state-level split available.

nodes.name: Permian (New Mexico portion)
nodes.notes: Physical sub-node; activated when state-level split available.

nodes.name: Bakken (North Dakota portion)
nodes.notes: ~90 percent of Bakken output.

nodes.name: Bakken (Montana portion)
nodes.notes: ~10 percent of Bakken output.

nodes.name: Eagle Ford (Texas)
nodes.notes: Eagle Ford is entirely within Texas; no PADD ambiguity.

nodes.name: Gulf of America (federal offshore)
nodes.notes: ~1.8 Mb/d in 2025. Reported as single EIA series.

nodes.name: Alaska North Slope
nodes.notes: Isolated subsystem. Feeds only TAPS.

nodes.name: California conventional production

configuration.notes: Kern County heavy oil + offshore state waters

nodes.notes: ~0.3 Mb/d; heavy-grade California crude.

configuration.notes: Includes SCOOP/STACK formerly reported as Anadarko

nodes.name: Permian basin (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Promoted to authoritative because EIA publishes only basin-level crude production.

nodes.name: Bakken basin (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Promoted to authoritative. Crosses PADD 2 / PADD 4.

nodes.name: Eagle Ford basin (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Trivially equals eagle_ford_tx; single-child observational aggregate for hierarchy consistency.

nodes.name: Permian-TX gathering network
nodes.notes: Collapsed under basin/region authoritative aggregate; activated when commercial gathering telemetry added.

nodes.name: Permian-NM gathering network
nodes.notes: Collapsed under basin/region authoritative aggregate; activated when commercial gathering telemetry added.

nodes.notes: Collapsed under basin/region authoritative aggregate; activated when commercial gathering telemetry added.

nodes.name: Eagle Ford gathering network
nodes.notes: Collapsed under basin/region authoritative aggregate; activated when commercial gathering telemetry added.

nodes.name: Alaska North Slope gathering
nodes.notes: Collapsed under basin/region authoritative aggregate; activated when commercial gathering telemetry added.

nodes.name: Midland origin terminal
nodes.notes: Largest Permian origin. Midland Basin side.

nodes.name: Wink origin terminal
nodes.notes: Delaware Basin side of the Permian.

nodes.name: Crane / Colorado City origin terminal
nodes.notes: Secondary Permian origin; older pipelines.

nodes.name: Johnson's Corner origin terminal
nodes.notes: Primary Bakken consolidation point.

nodes.name: Three Rivers / Karnes origin terminal
nodes.notes: Eagle Ford main consolidation.

nodes.name: Valdez Marine Terminal (TAPS terminus)
nodes.notes: TAPS pipeline terminus. Crude is loaded onto tankers here for shipment to West Coast refineries.

nodes.name: Cushing storage hub (aggregate)

configuration.operator: multiple (Enbridge, Plains, Enterprise, Magellan, Blueknight, SemGroup)

nodes.notes: WTI physical delivery point. Operator-level sub-terminals collapsed in starter.

nodes.name: Cushing â€” Enbridge terminal
nodes.notes: Activated when operator-level data available (e.g. Genscape, 10-K storage reports).

nodes.name: Cushing â€” Plains terminal
nodes.notes: Activated when operator-level data available (e.g. Genscape, 10-K storage reports).

nodes.name: Cushing â€” Enterprise terminal
nodes.notes: Activated when operator-level data available (e.g. Genscape, 10-K storage reports).

nodes.name: Patoka storage hub
nodes.notes: Midwest delivery hub for Bakken and Canadian crude.

nodes.name: Houston storage hub

configuration.operator: multiple (Enterprise ECHO, Magellan East Houston, Plains)

nodes.notes: Major Gulf Coast delivery and export feeder.

nodes.name: Corpus Christi storage hub
nodes.notes: Fastest-growing US export hub.

nodes.name: Nederland / Beaumont storage hub
nodes.notes: Major East Texas / Louisiana border hub.

nodes.name: St. James storage hub
nodes.notes: Louisiana delivery hub; LLS benchmark physical delivery.

nodes.name: SPR Bryan Mound

configuration.operator: US DOE Office of Petroleum Reserves

nodes.notes: Largest SPR site. Sweet crude; primarily draws onto Seaway or loads at Freeport.

nodes.name: SPR Big Hill

configuration.operator: US DOE Office of Petroleum Reserves

nodes.notes: Connected to Nederland hub and Beaumont marine terminal.

nodes.name: SPR West Hackberry

configuration.operator: US DOE Office of Petroleum Reserves

nodes.notes: Mixed sweet/sour. Connects to Lake Charles area.

nodes.name: SPR Bayou Choctaw

configuration.operator: US DOE Office of Petroleum Reserves

nodes.notes: Smallest SPR site. Connected to St. James hub.

nodes.name: SPR (aggregate view)
nodes.notes: Derived view; authoritative during weekly scenarios; not authoritative in monthly scenario.

nodes.name: Ingleside Energy Center (Corpus Christi)
nodes.notes: Largest US crude export facility. Added VLCC capability via reverse lightering.

nodes.name: Houston-area export terminals (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Aggregate over Seabrook and Houston Ship Channel docks. Sub-nodes collapsed.

nodes.name: Seabrook Logistics export
nodes.notes: Activated when facility-level export data available.

nodes.name: ECHO export dock (Enterprise)
nodes.notes: Activated when facility-level export data available.

nodes.name: Houston Ship Channel export (Magellan)
nodes.notes: Activated when facility-level export data available.

nodes.name: Nederland Sunoco export terminal
nodes.notes: Major Aframax-class export facility.

nodes.name: Louisiana Offshore Oil Port (LOOP) â€” bidirectional
nodes.notes: Sole US port capable of loading/discharging VLCCs directly. Both import and export flows.

nodes.name: Clearbrook, MN â€” Canadian pipeline entry
nodes.notes: Primary Canadian pipeline entry to US. Feeds PADD 2.

nodes.name: PADD 1 (East Coast) import terminals (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Aggregate East Coast imports; collapsed individual terminals.

nodes.name: PADD 5 (West Coast) import terminals (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Aggregate West Coast imports. Serves PADD 5 refineries, which import heavily.

nodes.name: Longhorn Pipeline
nodes.notes: Reversed in 2013 from products-eastbound to crude-eastbound.

nodes.name: DAPL / Energy Transfer Crude Oil Pipeline
nodes.notes: Dakota Access + Energy Transfer Crude Oil Pipeline combined system.

nodes.name: Trans-Alaska Pipeline System (TAPS)
nodes.notes: Heated pipeline. Currently operating well below nominal at ~500-700 kbpd.

configuration.operator: Harvest Pipeline Company (part of Exxon/Harvest JV)

nodes.name: EPIC Crude Pipeline
nodes.notes: Originally conceived as Permian-to-Corpus; repurposed to add Eagle Ford service.

nodes.name: Seaway Pipeline System
nodes.notes: Reversed in 2012 to carry crude southbound from Cushing glut; both directions still operational.

nodes.name: MarketLink Pipeline
nodes.notes: Gulf Coast extension of the Keystone system; Cushing to Nederland.

nodes.name: Capline Pipeline (reversed)
nodes.notes: Reversed in 2022 after decades of northbound service.

nodes.name: Spearhead Pipeline
nodes.notes: Carries Canadian heavy crude from Flanagan/Patoka area south to Cushing.

nodes.name: Enbridge Mainline / Flanagan South (US portion)
nodes.notes: Includes Mainline Clearbrook->Superior and Flanagan South segments. Ultimate throughput into Patoka/Cushing.

nodes.name: LOOP Onshore Connector
nodes.notes: Short high-diameter onshore connector between St. James hub area and LOOP's Clovelly salt-dome storage. Bidirectional: imports flow inland, exports flow offshore.

nodes.name: LOCAP / Ship Shoal crude lines
nodes.notes: Aggregated offshore pipelines (LOCAP, Ship Shoal system) connecting Houston-area infrastructure to LOOP for export loading.

nodes.name: PREMCOR REFINING GROUP INC — PORT ARTHUR
nodes.notes: Largest US refinery by capacity. Coker-equipped, processes heavy sour crudes.

nodes.name: VALERO REFINING CO TEXAS LP — TEXAS CITY
EXXONMOBIL REFINING & SUPPLY CO — BAYTOWN
nodes.notes: One of the most complex US refineries; integrated petrochemicals.

nodes.name: MARATHON PETROLEUM CO LP — GARYVILLE
nodes.notes: Receives Capline-reversed flow at St. James.

nodes.name: BP PRODUCTS NORTH AMERICA INC — WHITING
nodes.notes: Modernised 2013 to process heavy Canadian crude.

nodes.name: Marathon Los Angeles Refinery (Carson + Wilmington)
nodes.notes: Combined Carson + Wilmington operations after 2017 acquisition.

nodes.name: PHILLIPS 66 COMPANY — FERNDALE
nodes.notes: Receives Canadian crude via Trans Mountain Pipeline (not modelled).

nodes.name: PHILLIPS 66 COMPANY — LINDEN
nodes.notes: Tanker-fed; primary US East Coast refinery.

nodes.name: DELAWARE CITY REFINING CO LLC — DELAWARE CITY
Salt Lake City refining cluster

configuration.operator: HF Sinclair / Marathon / Chevron / Big West (combined)

nodes.notes: Aggregated cluster of 4 small Salt Lake refineries; processes Uinta/Wyoming sweet crudes.

nodes.name: HF SINCLAIR WYOMING REFINING CO — SINCLAIR
Guernsey storage hub (Wyoming)
nodes.notes: Major Rockies crude aggregator; Pony Express and Platte pipelines load here for shipment to Cushing (PADD2) and other markets.

nodes.name: Pony Express Pipeline (Guernsey to Cushing)
nodes.notes: Carries Wyoming/Bakken-adjacent crude from Guernsey south to Cushing.

nodes.name: Suncor Commerce City Refinery
nodes.notes: Sole Denver-area refinery; processes local DJ Basin / Wattenberg crude.

nodes.name: PHILLIPS 66 COMPANY — PONCA CITY
nodes.notes: Receives crude via Plains All American gathering systems + Cushing connections.

nodes.name: PADD 3 (Gulf Coast) import terminals (aggregate)
nodes.notes: Aggregate Gulf Coast crude imports â€” Mexican Maya, Saudi, Iraqi, Brazilian, Colombian heavy + medium sour tanker imports. ~2,300 kbd in 2023/2024.

nodes.name: Canadian Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin (aggregate)

basin_aggregation: Alberta oil sands (Athabasca, Cold Lake, Peace River) ~3,300 kbd
primary_grades: Western Canadian Select (WCS) â€” heavy sour bitumen blend
Cold Lake Blend (CLB) â€” heavy sour
Mixed Sweet Blend (MSW) â€” light sweet
Syncrude Synthetic Crude (SSX) â€” light sweet upgraded
primary_export_corridors: Trans Mountain TMX -> Burnaby BC -> tanker to PADD5 / Asia
configuration.operator: multiple Canadian E&P (Suncor, CNRL, Cenovus, Imperial Oil, MEG, etc.)

nodes.notes: Foreign production aggregate. Position pinned to Hardisty AB, the canonical Canadian crude hub where the Mainline, Keystone, and Express systems originate. Canadian production exits via the four cross-border pipeline systems modelled here.

nodes.name: Enbridge Mainline Canadian segment (Edmonton -> Clearbrook)
nodes.notes: Canadian segment of the Enbridge Mainline (Lines 1/2/3/4/13/65/67). Largest crude pipeline system in North America. Carries Western Canadian Select, Cold Lake Blend, and synbits from Edmonton/Hardisty down to the US border; continues as the existing pipe_enbridge_mainline node from Clearbrook MN onward.

nodes.name: Keystone Pipeline (Hardisty -> Cushing)
nodes.notes: The original Keystone Pipeline (not the cancelled Keystone XL extension). Carries heavy sour Canadian crude from Hardisty AB through Steele City NE down to Cushing OK. From Cushing, crude flows southbound on Marketlink and Seaway.

nodes.name: Trans Mountain Pipeline + TMX expansion (Edmonton -> Burnaby BC -> tanker)
nodes.notes: Trans Mountain Pipeline (original + TMX expansion completed May 2024). Edmonton -> Burnaby BC marine terminal at Westridge. The model abstracts the BC -> PADD5 tanker leg into the same node; output lands at padd5_imports_agg which then feeds Cherry Point and other PADD5 refineries. The TMX expansion tripled nameplate capacity to ~890 kbd.

nodes.name: Express + Platte Pipeline (Hardisty -> Casper -> Wood River)
nodes.notes: Express Pipeline runs Hardisty AB -> Casper WY (1,250 km, 280 kbd). Continues as Platte Pipeline -> Wood River IL. The model lands Canadian flow at Guernsey hub (close to Casper) so onward routing via Pony Express -> Cushing and Marketlink -> Nederland is captured by the existing pipeline network.

nodes.name: Texas non-Permian non-Eagle-Ford residual (East TX, Granite Wash, Barnett)

configuration.notes: Residual = MCRFPTX2 − permian_tx − eagle_ford_tx. Captures East Texas conventional, Granite Wash (panhandle), Barnett Shale (DFW), plus minor Cotton Valley / Austin Chalk volumes. Typical magnitude ~230 kbd (≈4% of TX state total).
Residual = MCRFPMT2 − bakken_mt. Predominantly Cedar Creek anticline (eastern MT) plus minor non-Bakken Williston volumes. Typical magnitude ~21 kbd.

PADD 1 production residual (NY/PA/WV/VA + small east coast)

attributes.notes: Captures unmodelled production in PADD 1 so the PADD production aggregate sums to the EIA-published total. Production formula: padd1_production_view minus the explicitly-modelled basins/states in PADD 1.

Captures unmodelled production in PADD 2 so the PADD production aggregate sums to the EIA-published total. Production formula: padd2_production_view minus the explicitly-modelled basins/states in PADD 2.

attributes.notes: Captures unmodelled production in PADD 3 so the PADD production aggregate sums to the EIA-published total. Production formula: padd3_production_view minus the explicitly-modelled basins/states in PADD 3.

Virtual boundary node representing the foreign destination of US crude exports. Symmetric counterpart of canadian_oil_sands (foreign source). Receives outflows from the 4 modelled PADD-3 export terminals; its consumption variable holds the modelled-PADD-3 export total (= MCREXP32, mirrored from padd3_exports_view).

Region balance aggregate. Carries production, consumption, inventory, balancing_item plus relational inflow/outflow to foreign boundary nodes and sibling regions. Variables aggregate from owned physical assets (basins, refineries, hubs, SPR sites) via sum_over_children, OR are TS-bound directly if region-level data is authoritative.

Abstract boundary node representing non-Canadian foreign crude imports into the US. Companion to canadian_oil_sands (Canada) and foreign_export_destination (export sink). Total foreign supply = canadian_oil_sands + foreign_supply outflows.

starter_coverage_contract.name: US_crude_starter_monthly_2015_2025
starter_coverage_contract.description: Starter coverage contract: EIA monthly data, crude oil only, January 2015 through December 2025.

eagle_ford_subsystem.notes: eagle_ford_tx is the authoritative production node; eagle_ford_gathering is a real intermediate node in the asset graph (analogous to permian_tx_gathering), not a collapsed alias.

refining_subsystem.notes: Individual refineries authoritative; PADD-level refining_centre_view is a derived rollup.

notes: SPR authoritative at site level, per thesis design decision (Chapter 5.3 validation target).
spr_total is therefore derived (formula = sum over four site nodes).
All physical nodes appear in the asset graph; collapsed nodes inherit flows from their authoritative ancestor at load time.
Refinery layer: 17 physical refineries authoritative at site level; 5 PADD-level refining_centre_view nodes are derived (sum of refinery C variables).
starter_coverage_contract.pipeline_layer_note: All 19 pipelines are authoritative at the named-pipeline level. Pump stations and intermediate injection points are not yet instantiated; they sit implicitly as collapsed future children in the resolution_hierarchy.
